channel.title: Comments for page "'Rogue AI' (NVEO4/YVN3E/PN6UG)"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "'Rogue AI' (NVEO4/YVN3E/PN6UG)"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote for the Rickroll

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno which is better, the 9.9/10 audio, or the 10/10 security footage.

+1.

can I upvote again? pretty please? No? COME ON

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did a Foundation Artificial Intelligence just rickroll Marshall Carter and Dark?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …From the entire content of the text conversation alone I'm guessing there was some GAW involvement, somehow.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded:
Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

Furthermore, the video in the last tab is unavailable due to DRM settings, on the off chance the author will ever read this and find another video mirror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In this particular case I copy pasted the code over from the MC&D hub for the various documentation types, and some have ltd. and some have LLP. That said I don't know how the Darke go in there. Might've grabbed that code from Bloodbaths (as it also has a single outlier "Darke") but I dunno.

Either way, they should all be fixed. Thanks for pointing it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
LLP stands for Limited Liability Partnership. It's essentially business jargon for the type of corporate structure Marshall, Carter & Dark uses.

And while it varies according to headcanon, there's an idea that MC&D has existed in various forms or iterations for many years. The evolution from Darke to Dark, or Ltd. to LLP in these GOI-formats can be used to hint at which stage or era of the organization that the note was made at. … Though unless I'm missing something, I think in this case the inconsistence may be accidental?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe I'm too peakyy, but for me it matters.

Why underlines sometimes states "Marshall, Carter and Dark, LLP" and sometimes "Marshall, Carter and Dark, Ltd.", and in one particular case "Marshall, Carter and Darke, Ltd."

They are the shareholders, is not they?

item.title: F'N GENIUS
item.content:encoded:
I like it, no, I love it.

I read this on my phone earlier and thought "hey it's pretty good." Now that I can listen to the audio and see the video, I BLOODY LOVE IT! The conversation (voice acting/dialogue) was good enough for me, but the Rick Roll was perfect, I mean I actually like that song so…I thought it was cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought this couldn't be that interesting and that I should probably skim most of the text, no matter how amusing the thought of high-tech nerds with a demon on fast dial for some occult science testing.

Then I read "Christmas peak" and "New Year's resolutions".

An author that tries to tell me that there's a Christmas peak in the artificial soul trading business is an author I like. An author that not only tells me there is a Christmas peak but a slightly smaller New Year's peak definitely has my full interest. Well done.

THE WORSHIPFUL ONE IS BONDED TO MY COUSIN ACTUALLY.

I take back what I said about the demon entity being amusing. Hr'asm'Kal is everything I want in a demon cooperating with humans, and amusing. This is some mundane-infused science fantasy jam and it tastes good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woops. Sorry for my confusion.

Still. I didn't expect to get Rick Roll'd here. Maybe in the forums, but not anywhere else. D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean it's tagged GOI because I had to, but as far as I'm concerned there's no real difference.

Still. Rick rolled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Especially on a tale page.

This is technically a GoI format. Some would say it is but a prettied up version of a Tale, but the tags 'tale' and 'goi-format' are mutually exclusive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The SCP Wiki is the last place I would expect to get Rick Roll'd. Especially on a tale page.

D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This used the format to pretty effectively tell the story, and definitely had me rooting for K-10, even if Grape is an obnoxious douche.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this concept, and I enjoyed the story told through the tabs. (The demon character was great in particular.) Really well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's some issues inherent to the whole concept of GoI formats that I have which appear to certain extents in this piece, the most pertinent of which being that these tend to be palette swaps of the mainlist article format. The technical nature of the company's records didn't quite feel like something kept by a secretive, amoral firm specializing in marketing atrocities to the wealthy and jaded.

With that out of the way, however, this was quite entertaining. The concept of farming out AIs as a means of a cheap substitute for human life, and some of the immediate parallels to real life issues, was a novel one, and really helps establish the tone for the forthcoming works on this MTF.

The rickrolling was a bit much, but then I suppose I could see a band of anomalous hackers finding that funny and doing it, so I can't really gripe about it. All in all, solid work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it was a pretty solid story - I'd probably have upvoted anyway. Then the Foundation rickrolled MC&D, and the upvote was mandatory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GDI Did I just get Rickrolled on the scp-wiki??

Anyway, great expansion on a great scip. you gets my +1.

Also, I never got a chance to appreciate the MC&D theme. Shit's gorgeous. Dat logo doe :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God damn. Its been awhile since I have been Rickrolled. What year is it?

In all seriousness, this was a fun read. It was nice taking a look into SCP-2987's backstory, and a bit of a thrill to see the behind the scenes developments at MC&D during all the fuss that surrounded that skip. Combined with some audio that I personally enjoyed very much, this has me looking forward to more Kappa-10 shenanigans. +1.

On a different note, shouldn't this have an Anderson Robotics tag?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this, finished it, and then went "That wasn't much. Why has this gotten so many upvotes?"

It was around then that I realized there were four more tabs. Now that I've actually finished it, I will say that this is really good. I'll also say that the voices aren't phenomenal, but they're functional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really love most of this.

When I ran this by several of our own tech people they ended up in a long discussion about philosophy. I'm not sure I followed all of it, but I am certain of this: they believe that they can create a converter that will allow artificial intelligences to be offered as sacrifices.

This is where I upvoted. The idea of sacrificing AI to gods is great, but the idea a bunch of engineers hearing they're acquiring AI, and immediately getting into a debate about if it's human enough to kill pushes it over the top.

Sender Hr'asm'Kal

Casual email with a demonic entity (that types in all caps)

Meet Alan. Twice a slave and always struggling for freedom. He has waged multiple campaigns to free his fellow AIs. His failures still weigh on his mind. You're buying a genuine freedom fighter, whose value is immeasurable to the right parties.

Casually talking about how well you enslaved somebody to make him more efficient for the buyer's killing needs.

The sound clip is meh but whatever we're not an acting site. The dialogue sounds meh too but I'd have to look at a script before deciding how much of that is due to the voice acting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hilariously, there's only one similar voice between those two articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddammit, that Rickroll.

I'll admit, I was kind of expecting more ritualized obeisance in the e-mails to the demon thing. I always like seeing that stuff. But given I more or less skipped the whole GoI contest, I really enjoyed reading this (and might not have given it a chance had I not seen Anderson on the front page). It's an interesting way to tell a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was pretty cool! But I have two little problems with it.

  • The demon's portrayal was kind of… stereotypical. Lots of harsh consonants and apostrophes, and typing in all caps, are kind of played out at this point :-P I'm sure you could come up with a more interesting way of having it obviously be a demon of some sort!
  • The voices in the audio log were too obviously the same as the ones in SCP-2284. Maybe it's unavoidable because of who has a good microphone or something, but that still made it hard for me to take seriously :-X

Otherwise, though, it was a fun story to read, so I'm going to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course demons send messages in all-caps.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy hell this looks amazing. Awesome job Cimmerian.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That security footage is really something. +1, damn you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tale tag was automatically added when I posted. It's also gone now. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't be both though. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GOI format. The tag's on now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The theme is borked on mobile. Image here. Might be more Randomini's thing to fix but I figure it's something to bring up nonetheless.

Also, just for record-keeping purposes, tale or GoI format?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Kaktus, Roget, LurkD and Astley for their help. Let the contest begin!

+ show block
– hide block

Just to note… if you take the numbers out of "'Rogue AI' (NVEO4/YVN3E/PN6UG)" you'll find it's an anagram for "Never Gonna Give You Up".

You're welcome.
